relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains trade execution records (equities, fixed income, derivatives) while the outbound file is a KYC (Know Your Customer) refresh request targeting individual customers. These serve fundamentally different purposes: trade records are transactional/operational data, while KYC refresh is customer identity/compliance verification. No meaningful field overlap exists—trade_records contain trader IDs, instruments, prices, and settlement details, whereas kyc_refresh_request contains customer personal data (DOB, national ID, email, phone) and KYC-specific compliance workflows. The systems are adjacent in a compliance pipeline, but the data domains are distinct and the outbound file's structure cannot be reasonably derived from the inbound file's structure.
